about.title: About me
about.subtitle: Learn about my journey, experiences, and passions that shape me as a developer

1.title: My Journey
1.textHtml:
Computer Engineering student at FESA (Faculdade Engenheiro Salvador Arena – SBC). I’m part of a personal learning project focused on quality so that, in the near future, beyond becoming an excellent professional, I can also share knowledge with those who need it most in a practical and easy-to-understand way—building applications that make technology users’ lives easier. To complement my skills, I’m constantly taking bootcamps, online courses, and participating in tech events about programming languages, software development, web development, and mobile development.

I have experience as a website developer, creating responsive layouts for different viewing platforms, building applications with object-oriented languages, working with agile methodologies, and developing projects in teams. In short: passionate about technology, dynamic, committed, with strong interpersonal skills, and always seeking to update my knowledge.

2.title: A bit more about my journey
2.textHtml:
When I was a kid, I loved taking apart and trying to reassemble electronic devices—like my mom’s stereo (there were always leftover parts and it usually meant a weekend grounded), video games, computers, and more. That’s where my interest in electronics came from. From 2002 to 2004 I attended CAI (Industrial Apprenticeship Course) in electrical training. Later, from January 2012 to December 2013, I started another chapter with a technical course in Industrial Electromechanics. From August 2014 to June 2017 I completed a degree in Industrial Mechatronics, where I had deeper contact with programming—it was love at first code.

Even though I was in love with programming, I decided to continue working in industrial maintenance, which was also part of my path.

But in 2020, during the pandemic, I had the opportunity to reconnect with programming and realized that’s where my heart truly was. With social distancing, I took online courses in programming logic, web development, mobile development, and databases. In August 2022, I began a Computer Engineering degree, and every day—together with courses and bootcamps—reinforces my passion for programming.

3.title: My Hobbies
3.textHtml:
It may sound unbelievable, but in my free time I love practicing sports like soccer (that Sunday pick-up game), hanging out with friends, and playing with my kids at the park (they have endless energy).

Of course, I can’t miss my programming time—always learning more and developing my skills. That’s when the hours fly by.

My partner often says that even when I claim I want to, I can’t stay away from coding (and of course I won’t say I want to, hahaha). It’s that desire that makes you wake up 30 minutes or even an hour earlier to get some coding in.

Between work, family, and studies, I try to use any free moment to mix things up—reading manga and playing games (in the past on console, now mostly on the phone).

intro.p2: If you have any suggestions regarding the website or my projects, please fill out the form, choose the “suggestion” option, and type your suggestion in the text area. I promise I will read it and take your opinion into account; and if it aligns with the website’s purpose, I will be happy to adopt it.
intro.p3: Currently, I work at a great company that provides cloud PACS solutions as a full‑stack web developer. However, I also have the flexibility to take freelance work. So, if you are interested in developing or maintaining a web application, please use the “budget” option in the form below and briefly describe what you need in the text area. With the information provided, I will get back to you for more details if necessary, and we can talk by email, WhatsApp, or schedule a meeting.
intro.p4: As I mentioned, I already have a job at a good company in a role I truly enjoy. Still, I believe the world is full of opportunities, and new experiences are a chance to grow. So it’s worth trying—perhaps I can be a strong fit for your company. For that, use the “proposal” option and write your proposal in the text area.
intro.p5: If you liked the website and the projects I have built so far, you can use the “compliment” option to leave a short compliment. Thank you very much—although it is genuinely enjoyable, development still takes work, because we need to keep learning and building new skills.
intro.p6: Many times we see applications and find some parts interesting. I have experienced that myself on several occasions. I believe our field needs more collaboration so we can keep improving our skills and create better applications every day, giving users the best possible experience. If that’s the case, use the “curiosity” option and write your question in the text area. I will be happy to help—or, if I don’t have an immediate answer, we will discover it together.
intro.p7: For all the cases above—and any other reason—you can fill out the form or contact me through the social network icons in the side menu or in the footer.
